learning_outcomes.description: Develop knowledge of the fundamental theory (concepts, statistical theory) and application of statistical data analysis.
Apply data analysis to your area of business research (e.g., management, marketing, strategy).
Conduct data analysis using linear regression and factor analysis.
Critically evaluate published research that uses multivariate data analysis techniques covered in the course.
Report the results of data analysis per discipline standards.
Critically discuss the validity of a research study, that uses any of the different types of multivariate data analysis techniques covered in this course, with an academic researcher.

assessments.task_description:
The report focuses on the completion of a computer exercise (Application of a Data Analysis technique) using software (e.g., SPSS). The data and topic on which students are to run the exercise will be provided in the class.

You will also be required to reflect on the principles/material covered and your learning experience in this course (first 6 weeks).

AI Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment will be submitted via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Critical Review of a Q1 level academic journal article of your choice (e.g., within your discipline), published after the year 2020, that employs Multivariate Methods (e.g., multiple regression or factor analysis). This assignment is designed to help you build confidence in reading and critically evaluating research that uses multivariate statistical methods. You will focus on assessing whether the analytical methods used in a published article are appropriate, correctly applied, and support the authors’ conclusions.

You will present review in class followed by a Q& A session.

Please Note: The assessment will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

AI Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Only in-class presentation, no written assignment due.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
